🎥: @hu.ma.ne

Ex-Apple employees just released this AI device. It runs on ChatGPT and it's going to replac
entries.caption:
1M likes, 5,810 comments - evolving.ai on November 10, 2023: "Humane's AI Pin wants to replace smartphones 🤯
🎥: @hu.ma.ne

Ex-Apple employees just released this AI device. It runs on ChatGPT and it's going to replace our phones. Here's how this could be the most important device since the iPhone:

• It projects visuals onto your hands instead of a screen
• You can control the device with hand gestures like pinching
• You can also control the device by talking to it directly
• Features include real-time voice translation, playing music, responding to calls and texts, and answering questions
• It runs on Humane’s Cosmos OS and OpenAI’s AI models
• It functions independently without a phone, which signals the company's intent to replace smartphones completely

The device shows the future of hardware: minimal devices that run on intuitive AI models, which can understand instructions and achieve complex tasks from our speech and hand gestures.

#ai #chatgpt #aitools #openai #aitips #machinelearning #midjourney".

Building upon traditional approaches, these eight specialized models advances AI’s ability to understand, reason, and generate across different domains and modalities.

Here’s architectures of these 8 state-of-the-art models:

1️⃣ LLMs (Large Language Models)
These foundational models process text token-by-token, enabling everything from creative writing to complex reasoning.

2️⃣ LCMs (Large Concept Models)
Meta’s newer approach encodes entire sentences as “concepts” in SONAR embedding space, transcending word-level processing.

3️⃣ VLMs (Vision-Language Models)
These multimodal combine visual and textual understanding to interpret images and generate text about them.

4️⃣ SLMs (Small Language Models)
Compact yet powerful models optimized for edge devices with tight energy and latency constraints.

5️⃣ MoE (Mixture of Experts)
These models activate only relevant expert networks per query, dramatically improving efficiency while maintaining performance.

Many users have noticed the model avoids conflict. It agrees too fast, softens every
entries.caption:
21K likes, 176 comments - chatgptricks on November 15, 2025: "ChatGPT just got a tougher personality.

Many users have noticed the model avoids conflict. It agrees too fast, softens every edge, and pads weak logic with comfort. Nice for feelings, bad for deep thinking.

A simple prompt flips that behavior. It turns ChatGPT into a direct adviser that challenges your ideas instead of validating them. It points out flaws, exposes excuses, and makes you face the real tradeoffs in your decisions. Some people pair this with long-term Memory so the model can track patterns and call you out when you repeat them.

This shifts ChatGPT from a cheerleader to something closer to a sparring partner.

Would you want your AI to be strict with you, or do you prefer the softer version?".

entries.caption:
2,184 likes, 2,643 comments - charlieautomates on February 2, 2026: "Comment “AI Agent” for the FREE Subagent Worksheet.

If you’re using Claude Code without intentional subagents, you’re wasting most of its power.

The problem: one long main chat burns context fast and muddies thinking.

The fix: offload work to subagents so your main session stays clean and strategic.

Parallel subagents run multiple tasks at once and report back together.
Sequential subagents build step by step, each one improving the last.

Train Claude with a custom MD file so it knows when to suggest parallel vs sequential and how to execute each.

That’s how you go from “asking for help” to actually operating Claude like a system.

Results: cleaner context, better outputs, and way more leverage per prompt.

#AItools
#ClaudeCode
#AIworkflow
#Anthropic
#Automation".

entries.caption:
1,573 likes, 26 comments - thisweekinstartups on February 4, 2026: "Top 5 Openclaw (Clawdbot )skills! The team here at TWiST has been all over Openclaw. Here’s 5 examples of how we’ve used it to boost our production work flows on the pod!

1. Elevelabs

We had AI find and summarize 5 different youtube videos! From sourcing to transcribing, Openclaw did it all!

1. "Task Monitor"

This skill allows you to see all bots running on your system in one place. This is a potential game changer to help address some of the security concerns people have with Openclaw.

Instead of writing one long request, they break complex tasks into smaller steps that validate each other before moving forward. Instead of hoping the format stays consistent, they enforce structure so outputs remain reliable every time.

They also separate system rules from user input to keep behavior consistent, adjust temperature based on the type of task, and add self checks before the final answer is returned.

Google has launched an early preview of Web Model Context Protocol (WebMCP) with Chrome 146, aimi
entries.caption:
1,915 likes, 18 comments - appinventiv4ai on February 11, 2026: "Google introduces WebMCP to make websites AI-native

Google has launched an early preview of Web Model Context Protocol (WebMCP) with Chrome 146, aiming to make websites directly accessible to AI agents.

WebMCP allows sites to expose structured, machine-readable tools instead of relying on fragile techniques like screen scraping or DOM inference. According to Google’s design document, the protocol creates a clear “contract for interaction” between web apps and AI agents.

It supports imperative tool registration through the navigator.modelContext API, as well as declarative HTML annotations. This means agents can reliably interact with forms, buttons, and complex workflows without hacks or automation layers.

Developers see this as a major shift. Today, browsing agents often rely on Chrome DevTools Protocol or automation frameworks like Playwright to operate the browser. With WebMCP, agents could interact with Chrome more directly, removing a layer of workaround infrastructure.

If adopted widely, this could redefine how AI agents navigate and use the web.

entries.caption:
12K likes, 6,451 comments - theaisurfer on March 26, 2026: "Combining Claude Code with NotebookLM is a complete cheat code that nobody's talking about

🔥 Comment "SETUP" for everything.

Here's how to set up your own research agent that costs you nothing and runs locally:

1. Clone the notebooklm-py repository from GitHub
2. Install the requirements with pip install -r requirements.txt
3. Run playwright install chromium to get the browser driver
4. Add the included skill file to your Claude Code setup
5. Start feeding sources to your new research agent

This combo gives you a research assistant that can pull together slide decks, infographics, and comprehensive reports in minutes. NotebookLM handles the RAG processing while Claude Code automates the entire workflow through the terminal.

The setup takes about two minutes and your data stays completely private since everything runs on your machine.

🔥 Comment "SETUP" for everything.

#ai #automation #research #claude #productivity".

entries.author: theaisurfer
entries.title: Claude Code + NotebookLM local research agent workflow
entries.caption: Post describes combining Claude Code with notebooklm-py to create a local research assistant that can generate reports, slide decks, and infographics.
entries.summary: The reel proposes a 5-step setup: clone notebooklm-py, install Python requirements, install Playwright Chromium, add a Claude Code skill file, then feed sources into the workflow. The key pitch is getting a fast research pipeline (RAG + terminal automation) while keeping data local.
entries.whyItMatters: This is a practical pattern for teams that want AI research automation without sending sensitive data to external SaaS pipelines. It can reduce research turnaround time while preserving privacy and local control.
actions: Test the notebooklm-py setup in an isolated environment and validate dependency compatibility.
Create a reusable input template for sources (links, PDFs, notes) to standardize research runs.
Benchmark output quality across 3 tasks: summary report, slide draft, and infographic brief.

entries.author: olivermerrick___
entries.title: Claude Code + Excalidraw MCP to visualize agent architectures
entries.caption: Creator explains a 3-step workflow to install Excalidraw MCP, add a custom skill file, and ask Claude Code to draw architecture diagrams after each task.
entries.summary: The post shows a practical workflow: after building with Claude Code, generate an Excalidraw diagram that maps agents, APIs, databases, and data flow. The key claim is that a dedicated skill file improves diagram quality (clear structure, labeled nodes, meaningful flow) versus generic auto-diagrams.
entries.whyItMatters: As projects grow, architecture visibility becomes the bottleneck. Visualizing what the agent built improves debugging, onboarding, and review quality, and helps catch broken data paths early.
actions: Create a reusable prompt: 'Create an Excalidraw diagram of what you just built, including components, dependencies, and data flow.'
Standardize a diagram checklist: inputs, outputs, storage, external APIs, failure points.
Require architecture diagram export for major automation tasks before closing them.
